Module 4:
Creating Prelaunch Content,
Part 4
!
Executive Summary
!
This section will be all about creating full motion videos. Just like with the
screencap video you need to have a good script.
The effectiveness of your full motion video is going to vary with your talent,
i.e. you. People want to see you - they don’t want to see some fancy
professional actor.
You need start where you are and then you just get better from there. It is
much easier to do full motion video with a teleprompter. This fits in front of
your camera, and it will have your script on the camera.
If you want to shoot outside, teleprompter’s really don’t work very well.
There are some that work outside but they tend to be a lot more expensive.
Some people use green screens (I never have). This is agreen piece of
fabric. From there you can insert a fake background instead of that green
screen in the editing process.
Full motion video is generally harder to edit. but if you get a good editor
they can go through it quickly. It’ also possible to do it yourself quickly - I’ve
done Q&A videos for PLC’s.
!

-- 1 of 3 --

The important thing to remember is that it’s not about the camera. The most
important thing is you, how you appear on the camera, and how you deliver
your content.
The second most important thing is getting the lighting right and getting the
set right. The third most important thing is then the camera. You can even
use your smartphone for this!
External audio input is very important. If your camera does not have an
external audio input, then you can record audio with an external device.
Then in your editing match up the audio and the videos
Another piece to consider is audio monitoring. This is really nice to have,
but tends to be in more advanced cameras. Who ever is shooting your
video can actually plug in a headset and make sure that the audio you’re
capturing is good.
Why? Sometimes you’ll record and not know the audio you’re recording is
poor until afterward.
• 	Camera choices
• 	Your phone
• 	We have used Canon Vixias in the past
• 	We’re now using Digital SLR’s (DSLR’s)
• 	Canon 5D and a Canon 6D - very expensive, professional option
• 	Remember...it’s not about the camera
!
2	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 2 of 3 --

As I mentioned, audio is super important. If you’re doing an outside shot,
watch out for the wind noise.
You can get lavalier mics, these come in wired and wireless. You can find
some starting at just $30. We currently use a Sennheiser EW112PG2 (very
expensive).
• 	Teleprompter
• 	Very nice to have if only for the bullet points
• 	They can be expensive
• 	You can make your own with an iPad
• 	Go to Google and search iPad teleprompter
Also, it’s important to use a tripod, and it doesn’t have to be fancy. Even a
$30 tripod is going to be a hundred times more effective than not using one
at all.
• 	Editing software - lots of choices
• 	Mac - iMovie (free), Adobe Premiere, Final Cut
• 	Windows - Adobe Premiere, Sony Vegas, and many others
Next I’m going to walk you through some of the other types of prelaunch
content
!
